name: video-playback-tracker
description: A cross-browser extension for **Chrome** and **Firefox** that automatically tracks your video playback timestamps on any streaming website — so you never lose your place again.

scripts.build: npm run build:extension && npm run build:web
scripts.sync-manifests: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const mx = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('manifest.json')); const mf = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('manifest.firefox.json')); ['version', 'permissions', 'host_permissions', 'action', 'icons'].forEach(k => mf[k] = mx[k]); fs.writeFileSync('manifest.firefox.json', JSON.stringify(mf, null, 2));"
scripts.build:firefox: npm run build:extension && npm run sync-manifests && mkdir -p dist/firefox && cp manifest.firefox.json dist/firefox/manifest.json && cp dist/background.js dist/content.js dist/popup.js popup.html popup.css dist/firefox/ && cp -r icons dist/firefox/
scripts.package: npm run build:firefox && cd dist/firefox && zip -r ../../dist-firefox.zip .
scripts.package:source: zip -r source.zip src/ manifest.json package.json README.md
scripts.build:all: npm run package && npm run package:source
